spec_match.rationale: Removal feature — page covers one slice (probe / migration / note) but the spec's full removal+replacement story is split across multiple concepts; this page can't carry it alone.

interactive.rationale: Has 2 interactive control(s) wired to JS but the surface is thin — a single button or readout is barely past the static-card threshold.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on apis, both, compare, frame).

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5162221567082496.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: showing what fails after removal
openQuestions.detail: The deprecate-and-remove-document-requeststorageaccessfor feature's headline framing — document.requestStorageAccessFor removal — top-level sites cannot request cross-site cookie access on behalf of embeds — calls out "showing what fails after removal". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: Storage Access API (rSA) as the replacement
openQuestions.detail: The deprecate-and-remove-document-requeststorageaccessfor feature's headline framing — document.requestStorageAccessFor removal — top-level sites cannot request cross-site cookie access on behalf of embeds — calls out "Storage Access API (rSA) as the replacement". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 2 button(s), 0 input(s), 0 select(s), 0 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: rsa-vs-rsa under deprecate-and-remove-document-requeststorageaccessfor: 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
